Meeting with Jennifer to review her export readiness assessment results. I spoke with her and her husband Greg regarding their business model. They restore and maintain vintage audio equipment. People from across the world send them items to restore/maintain and they ship items back to the sender once the item is finished so they do not export those items. Sometimes they will have a customer request electrical components for self-install and ship those with the main item, these products would be considered exports so I spent some time searching their harmonized code for export licenses or tariffs and sent him a follow up email after the meeting. We briefly reviewed the assessment which they filled out more as a perceived requirement to speak with me about their “exporting” questions. They scored a 49 and could be a good candidate for exporting with some planning if they had a product they sold consistently. Since their shipments are “returned goods” they are technically not exportin

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Meeting with Darlene Brown, 04/02/2025


Darlene provided an update on the recent arrival of a new resident at her facility and discussed the current staffing situation, highlighting the need for budget adjustments until full occupancy is achieved. She is in the process of obtaining estimates for necessary renovations, including a fence and bathroom updates, but has only received verbal quotes so far. The installation of an automatic stair lift has also presented challenges due to the home's age and layout, which they are working to address.


Darlene detailed her demanding caregiving responsibilities, which require her to be available around the clock for wellness checks. Although she has scheduled five appointments this month to provide some daytime relief, the continuous nature of her duties has led to sleep deprivation. She is actively seeking support from organizations like Tri County on Aging and is focused on building relationships to increas

student entrepreneur, Bovei, who is developing an app to help BA researchers with organization and management.

Here's a summary of the key points:

  • Customer Discovery: Nancy emphasizes the importance of proper customer discovery, focusing on identifying the most urgent needs of a specific target audience. She advises against broad targeting and stresses the need to prioritize "ones and twos" (high urgency) over "threes" (nice-to-haves) in terms of problem urgency.
  • Interview Process: Nancy outlines a specific interview structure:
    • Walk me through a day in your...
    • What are the problems, weaknesses, challenges, and obstacles?
    • What's working well?
    • What are the top three things that need to be addressed to try something new?
    • How urgent are those three things (on a scale of 1-5)?
  • Iterative Development: She advocates for an iterative

1. Reviewed comments on the business. She needs to gather more accurate details on the selling price, renovations and other start up expenses. She couldn't commit to how much she has to invest.
  2. Discussed multiple member LLC versus single member LLC and how this differs from a partnership at the local level. I advised she seek legal advice with the operating agreement for a multiple member LLC.
  3. Discussed being on payroll versus owner draw. They plan to keep their full-time jobs and reinvest in the business over the next few years.
  4. Recommended she create financial projections using a spreadsheet to create a cash flow and income statement. I shared how the tool works by putting in random numbers to show how the tool works. She has the spreadsheet from a previous email from me and is somewhat familiar with Excel so will begin working on this.
  5. Discussed Seller Financing. She will discuss when she meets the owner if this is an option.

Sam Augusta

JD is navigating the challenges of refining his pitch deck for Gymforce while staying true to his long-term vision of building a durable, category-defining company rather than aiming for a quick exit. He’s working to convey the dual-sided value of the platform for both independent gyms and small businesses while positioning against larger players like Wellhub. JD also wants to show momentum and traction, but needs to streamline his messaging, better illustrate market rollout, and build a clearer go-to-market and revenue strategy in his deck.

I recommended focusing the pitch more tightly, especially in the first three minutes, emphasizing traction, customer outcomes, and business potential. I encouraged JD to simplify the story—shortening the intro, removing jargon like “honeymoon wore off,” and aligning the slides with a clean narrative arc: problem, solution, business model, traction, market strategy, and growth potential. I also suggested replacing the flywheel slide with a

sent business plan template to begin creating the pitch layout for sponsors/investors. Marketing of Retreat - July 18-20-2025

A MESSAGE FROM RITA HILLMAN AT THE SMALL BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT CENTER

Rita Hillman
Intro email and called

I met with Jesse Corbett yesterday and we covered a lot of ground, but I think we should focus on four areas: identifying business opportunities, understanding who his competitors are, determining what his startup costs are going to be and completing the business plan. More importantly however, I would like for him to get a feel for how these stands operate, which is why he should reach out to three hot dog vendors outside his service area to get a feel for their business. He can reach them through the local Chambers of Commerce or google, and use the information for his Competitive Analysis. Or he can go on YouTube and view the videos on How to Set Up a Successful Hot Dog Vending Business or learnhotdogs.com. There's excellent information about the set up, where to set up, etc. which should be valuable. I also recommend the following:

  1. In identifying business opportunities, contact local middle schools and high schools (Principal or Assistant Principal or Administrati

Analysis: William is still focused on his shirt business. Since our last meeting, he has made significant progress by attending a meeting for housing trust fund development, joining the SBAM and participating in a leadership class, which he thoroughly enjoyed. Additionally, he has been accepted into the Thrive program, which will begin next week.

Recommendation: To move forward, William should complete his brand organizer to clearly define his business identity and narrow down his target audience. This will help him focus his marketing efforts and refine his business strategy.

Next Steps:

  1. Complete the brand organizer to establish a clear brand identity.
  2. Define his target audience to ensure his marketing is targeted and effective.
  3. Complete the personality exercise to align his brand’s tone and message with his audience.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Diane sent me the below email frustrated that she feels her meeting this afternoon is not going to be good. She is meeting with her assigned Champion Allison, Tonya with MSHDA and Rachel who is a new Champion. I recommended some calming techniques for Diane, like deep breaths prior to the meeting. I also encouraged her to think more positive about the purpose of the meeting and not make assumptions that they are going to drop her from the program. I asked Diane the following questions:

  1. Did they say they are dropping you from the program and that is the purpose of the meeting? She stated no. I asked for her to read the emails she received that lead up to scheduling the meeting? It appears Allison had asked Diane for specific information be sent to her, once Allison received the information she stated she was ready to schedule a meeting with her. I shared my thoughts and interpretation of this is not negative but possibly positive and they are now moving the convers

I read through notes as prep the client canceled the appointment.

Client received the contract for a large bid that will keep them busy for the next year or two. They will reach back out in the future should they need help with their business. Closing account, account can be reopened in the future should the client return looking for assistance.
The request is now complete.

Joseph reached out to ask for my help in getting things moving for this year.


He had prepared his business plan with some longer range goals and I wanted to make sure that we approached them appropriately.


He has seen some damage due to the storms. I explained that the EIDL program should become available and that he should record and itemize any losses due to the storms.


We discussed his target opening date on the course of May 1 and the desire to have new equipment in place to help manage the golf course. I explained that he needs to focus his plans on getting a loan and should be ready to define and cost the equipment he wants to buy.


He let me know that he has joined Golf Now and the Golf Network and expects to attract more players. He has also gone digital so that golfers can reserve tee times online.


He explained that he cannot afford to buy new equipment due to the cost but believes that good

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM: She needs general help getting her organization underway.

 

RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN: She sent me her draft completion of Form 502...which I reviewed. Needs a few things still.

 

NEXT STEPS & FOLLOW-UP ACTIONS: We will meet to complete this form

 

IMPACT REMINDER: We talked about the economic impact of her efforts

Follow up meeting with JC and Nicole to review their business plan. We walked through the plan and I gave feedback. There was a lot of information he added that didn’t make sense or wasn’t relevant to his business. We worked through those areas and went over the outline again identifying areas of relevance on which to focus. He met with a web developer from the list I sent and they were trying to get him to start a whole new website on Wordpress because they do not deal with Shopify sites. I recommended he speak with someone else about getting his website the bare necessary to facilitate bike sales, rentals and repairs. We will meet in a couple weeks to work on the budget after he has a chance to apply my feedback from today.

Client is stressed and exacerbated with the financial situation around buying the restaurant and needed to talk it out. He has not talked to the owner, I suggested he have a real conversation and find out what the owner wants for a payment on the land contract. I suggested the bank might be willing to make a loan if the owner would take second position on the loan. We'll meet again next week.

I just want to communicate directly, I hope this doesn't come off as rude (although I understand I am being critical in this email). At this time, I am going to step back from working with the SBDC. Hi Juliette,

Let me know if you need help developing your accountability chart. I do that with clients a lot.

Here is an example with some explanation at the beginning (cut and pasted from the book):

Thx - db

Accountability Chart

The rule: It’s “ok” for a person to be accountable for more than one function, but it’s not OK to have more than one person accountable for the same function.

FIT Test = Y/N

FULFILL: Can they fulfill the demands of the position, both now and in the future? Do they have both the physical (time) and mental capacity to get the job done? This is an especially important question if one person – like the owner – is trying to fill more than one seat.

INTEGRATE: Can they integrate with the team? Do they understand the importance of the position to the company as a whole? Do they understand how their role should interact with the rest of the organizat

Summary: The client sent the NIH Specific Aims that she will send to the program manager. She asked for a review and to let her know if this is sufficient for an initial discussion. 


Action: I have taken a look at the document that they sent me and I think it is in great shape. I made some minor corrections in the document itself and sent it to her. The main suggestion that I have is that they need to state somewhere in here that they are working with partners on this. While I wouldn’t necessarily suggest this for the final proposal specific aims section (it comes elsewhere in the proposal), I believe it would help the program manager understand that they will be working with a university(s) on getting this done. It helps to understand that there is a significant medical setting behind this. It could be a separate subsection that they can remove later, or it can be a part of their intro. This is especially important if they are going to do an STTR so they can see th

Summary: The client wrote from what she can tell, the NSF is allowing for phase I submissions, although it's currently quite vague about how things are impacted by new executive orders. She wanted to know what I have heard, and what I would recommend. Their thought is just to submit and hope that earlier submission will increase chances of funding.


Action: I responded that as of right now, for the NSF everything seems to be “as is”. No significant cuts to budget so no cut to the program for them. There is some fear that ultimately universities will have some problems, but currently that is not in the NSF awards which is keeping its budget available to SBIR/STTR in tact. There was a debacle with university review panels though that was quickly rectified and reestablished. The administration has their current focus on other agencies right now. NSF has its di minimus indirect cost rate at 15% already so there is no anticipated changes to that like there was for NI

The client would like to start a data services agency in Michigan that focuses on reporting and visualization. Doing our meeting we discussed the following:


1. Define Your Niche & Services

 Decide on your target industries (e.g., healthcare, finance, manufacturing) and the specific services you’ll offer, such as:

  • Data Reporting (custom reports, dashboards)
  • Data Visualization (interactive charts, graphs, BI dashboards)
  • Data Analysis & Insights (trends, forecasting)
  • Automation (streamlining reporting processes)

2. Develop Your Business Plan

Outline:

  • Market

Analyzed M25 Ventures suitability from web research and Pitch Book entry, including average deal size, number of recent investments, dry powder available, partners' company involvement, number of board seats, historical performance of funds, profile of fund sizes over time, IRR, exits, live companies in portfolio, regional connections. Will get independent references and discuss these results with the CEO on our next Zoom call.

I met with our client by telephone. Our client switched projects and would now pursue creating an aerial drone service primarily for agricultural applications. However the service would also be able to be used for quite a few other applications as well. She envisions employing 3-4 people and purchasing drone and support equipment costing about $150k. She is scheduled to begin her drone training next month through their union.

She would likely need to set up the LLC, seek financing, purchase the drones and other equipment in order to begin the licensing and certification process which she expect would take up to six months once the drones are purchased. The licensing and certifications would be drone specific. I sent her our Shareable Business Model and will give her access to LivePlan in due time. Email sent: https://michigansbdcatgra

Feedback on slide deck from Scott Taylor. Recommends defining the problem in more competition comparison terms, detailing the difference from what is currently not working. Also to avoid using numbers so as to avoid triggering an analytic, critical mind set. Advocates market sizing from beachhead to end state ten year goal including all verticals, and staying within rough bands.

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

Client owns Dearborn area barber shop (inbusiness for 70 years) and has an opportunity to open a second shop in Canton, Cherry Hill Village. The space was a barber shop previously and the previous owner went out ob business in Jan. 2025. It appears to be turnkey with just some minor cosmetic updates and new signabe.

RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

Do the due diligence with the State licensing and the neighboring businesses. Check with Twp planning and DDA for opportunities and issues. Get an attorney to assist with lease negoations. Start looking for staff for the new location, consider a short term deal with existing staff to help until team can be hired (Independe

Client has been contacted by Bronson Hospital's senior care center and asked to bid on washing their linens. This would be approximately 250,000 lb of linen. Client went thru the numbers to me-I encouraged client to add a little knowing Bronson will want to quibble with whatever price he will offer

Scott Hotz is navigating the early stages of launching his battery technology startup, focused on a dendrite-preventing separator that enables fast-charging, cold-operation, and safer lithium metal batteries. His key challenges include clarifying the best go-to-market strategy, choosing between funding options (non-dilutive grants vs. angel investment), and effectively communicating the product's value in a pitch deck. Scott is also grappling with uncertainty around investor readiness and how deeply to dive into technical explanations while still building excitement.

I recommended that Scott revise his pitch to follow a 3-minute product/7-minute business format, with a focus on building excitement rather than simply educating. We discussed refining the pitch deck structure to include simplified visuals, a clearer "Why Now" slide with current market trends, and a go-to-market timeline that highlights niche beachhead markets like battery energy storage and defense.

Co-consult with Nate Carr. We talked through some of the financials and realize the information we have is his financial projection numbers without having historical numbers (2023, 2024) as reference points. Evan will be working on those and sending them to us for review. Client is struggling with enrollments now that he is charging $50 (vs free). Suggested he create a membership fee of $10/month for the season to make it easier for parents to afford. Suggested doing a crowdfunding as well to cover the cost of some of the kid's equipment (helmet, glove, bat?). Evan considering all of it. Nate and I will also review the written plan and determine if starting in Kalamazoo (vs Battle Creek) is a better strategy this year since there are many more kids to reach and the target audience in Kalamazoo may have a little more disposable income. meeting again in about 2 weeks.

Discussed the work he did with the township board, ways of potentially being pad.

Business Planning call using Live Plan. I had already used the data Karyn sent over to populate the business plan. We went through it, and the financials. She is going to tidy up some of the language, work on her pricing, calculate start-up costs, and learn more about the regulations and licensing.


She may need a loan to get this started. There is room in this plan to profit, but she needs to make sure she is thinking of all of her expenses. She is also only planning to pay her staff (including herself0 $15/hr. She may have room to pay more. The rent may also not be $5k month. TBD.


I am giving her until Monday to make the changes, then I will print the PDF and send it to her.
Sent Cliff business plan template, training resources, and basic bookkeeping template. He will complete plans for both business ideas and send to me for feedback.

Renee has been trying to figure out how to get her idea into a format where she can open her business. She's frustrated. I learned that there are a lot of question marks that still need answers, which is why she's frustrated. She's working on her credit score and would like to buy a home or homes to house people for 6-12 months to give them time to get back on their feet. She's passionate about the homeless, those aging out of foster care and returning citizens.


She's not exactly sure how to make everything work, so we talked through the areas that are missing information and how to go about gathering it.

Next Steps: Continue attending networking events to attract new clients and further expand her professional network.

Met at the restaurant site. Looked over her financials on LIvePlan. Her main question is where does her loan payment show up on her financials. Advised against making business purchases from personal credit cards.

Vent hood for kitchen ($60,000+) puts her over her budget. She stated that she could have taken a larger loan to cover this. Discussed options. The contractor said she could pay half and the other half in installments. Not having the kitchen vent means she cannot have the full menu she was hoping to have. Discussed options including opening with a limited menu. She showed me the more limited menu and it still looks like a decent-sized menu. Discussed marketing plan as well.

NEXT STEPS: Meet soon to go over financials in more depth. Perhaps Daniel can join.

Met with Pam at the Restaurant.

Emphasized once again looking at ways to bring down COGS and Labor expense (Prome Cost).

Also, suggest looking more closely at Food truck costs and creating separate a P&L statement for the truck. They expect to hit many events including BridgeFest and Chassell Strawberry Festival.

Looked at Marketing. Website, Google Profile, and exterior of the restaurant. Marketing is quite good on a limited budget. Suggested informal partnership with Alpha Brewery as Pam noted that many customers order food and take it to the brewery. I suggested agreeing with Alpha Brewery to refer them for customers looking for alcoholic beverages and the Brewery will likewise recommend them for food. She seemed to like the idea.

Hi Siera,

Things are going good! I have been very busy with my day job and some recent orders! I just got news this week that I got accepted into the market I've been wanting to get into this summer! I am pretty excited about that, I am hopeful it will be a good market for me and maybe I will gain some regulars.

In other news:

I'm still looking into business bank accounts. I think I'm gonna focus on on that and get one opened this month!

For my Facebook & Instagram marketing I am trying to post more consistently! I am currently thinking about my Easter menu and what all I should offer. Sometimes I think I give people too many choices. I have been doing a lot of cakes lately. I enjoy cakes but am not fully confident in them yet! So hoping maybe to stay more cookie or cupcakes geared for my Easter menu! I'll attach the 2 cakes I just did over the weekend as well!!

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Zachariah John Thompson
Lance Wolfe <lwolfe@mtu.edu>

Met with Jim to discuss progress with investor relations and questions he is getting. One of the biggest challenges is concerns that the market size and potential revenues stream is not significant enough. Discussed business model, and brainstormed ideas on additional revenue streams, including ongoing support for at-home training, more frequent check-ins on progress, and expansion into other markets outside of Parkinson's. James will meet with team to investigate these options and update the deck accordingly.

Meeting with Isaiah for credit counseling and loan packaging advice. He was declined for SBA loan due to credit score, but Paul from SNBT otherwise expressed interest in the loan. Isaiah is considering using a lot of cash to do a share buyout and land contract with his brother. Discouraged this, as he should be getting bank debt financing to preserve his cash on hand. Reminded him to update LLC filings for Midwest Munitions. Sent him proposed structure and ideas for continuing with SNBT:

Laurie Lonsdorf and I met with Simone this morning. She was out of town for personal reasons during our last scheduled time so this was an opportunity to check in and see how things were going with the business, repricing her merchandise, and the upcoming Spring Fling event. Simone shared that she had been on a bit of an emotional rollercoaster over the last week and was not feeling confident in herself. She has gotten the merchandise repriced, and is getting ready to mark down her winter pieces for clearance after she goes to Flint to pick up new Spring pieces.


As an introvert, Simone said that she struggles with being the face of the business, locally, but is committed to making it a success. She recently met with another business owner locally for some advise and they recommended that she reach out to the Boys and Girl Club about getting a part time student. Simone will need to take some extended time off for a person

Keith is an inventor and wants to manufacture and sell a modular picture frame.


 


Today at Dunkin Donuts

Allison is no longer assigned to Diane and she was introduced to Rachael, which Diane said was very pleasant. Tonya lead the meeting and they discussed and resolved both of her issues.

  1. If her tenant does not move out that is earning more than she should to qualify for low income housing, than Diane just can qualify for that $40,000. However, Diane may not need to use the full so this may not have an impact on her project.
  2. Diane can use the new bid and add more windows, she just cannot add the siding. However, because the bid came in lower, Diane is still approved for the full amount and she can use her portion to pay for the siding, if she wants to do this.

Does Juan need any additional support? (cc'd Diego)

Happy Friday Juan,

Is there more we can do to assist you at this point? We're happy to answer additional questions or provide more resources, if helpful at this time.


If you'd like to schedule another session, we can do so - please select a time that works best for you and we'll continue the conversation.

• Attended WCC business expo- grabbed an appt on my calendar.
  • Working with Millie in cohort program.
  • Business is started now and she had her first sale of $150, for 6 hours of work she is not charging enough and is concerned. Discussed tools for improvement, sent survey to gather impact on progress made. Advised her to open bank account and keep biz trans seperate. Suggested that her mother help her potentially get the busienss up and running.
  • Material purchased from Nigeria and making custom peices here, 15 yards produces two blazer.

Lindsay got a PT job working for in-home assistance for the elderly. This is a great foundation for her business. Shared her financial information and it is a good start. We'll take that and get it into the 36-month projections and she'll work more on the existing written plan she's developed. Next session will cover the written plan and in the following session from that, we'll file for the LLC.

prep time reviewing BP + call with John Schmitt

call with client as plans have shifted in last few days:

found, purchased, and applied for a Special Designated Merchant liquor license. 


The short of it is, we were going to provide complementary wine tasting during music nights but apparently that is a felony (what?!). So I made some calls, asked for some opinions, and just like that (in a matter of about 5 hours) I had a signed contract to purchase a SDM license and an investor to front the money. All the paperwork was submitted to the MLCC today. 


ALSO, I have been made aware of a local group called Common Cents Capital. They lend money to local triple-bottom line enterprises. They define that as small businesses that are targeting financial, social, and environmental returns. I am applying for a $30,000 loan with them instead of 4Front. This is a far more personal opportunity and I know two members of their board, both of which heavily

Notes


﻿Target Audience: Client will define their ideal customer (age, interests, shopping habits) to refine marketing strategies.

Etsy Ad Test: Recommended starting with a small budget ($2–$5/day) for Etsy Offsite Ads or Promoted Listings, running for 7–10 days.

Ad Setup: Sent over resource to review setting up Etsy Ad.

Previous Campaign Data: Client to review and send (optional) any past campaign data (from Faire, etc.) to help inform strategy; no data is fine, client can start fresh.

Focus: Priority is defining the target audience and running the Etsy ad test. We'll revisit Pinterest and other strategies later. Opposed to any other social media platforms unless it is outsourced.

Support: Client is encouraged to reach out for help with any steps, and I’ll provide guidance as needed.</

Andrew/Lance webinar with client; worked on 2025 reconciliation of bookkeeping as start date; prior years were handled by CPA. We used the bank statements for January 2025 (client wanted to connect bank to WAVE) and went thru the Jan. reconciliation. There were over 100 transactions so client will now continue to work on checking off the bank statement/WAVE statement and if he can balance for January 22025, will continue entering/reconciling ,but suggested if Jan. does not balance that we work together next week prior to moving into Feb..

I spoke with Melanie, who is an employee of, and handles all of the administrative work for, Iron Horse Consulting LLC. Iron Horse is a Fractional CFO company based in Utah. The owner of the single member LLC is Dana, and she is moving to MI. She wants to transfer the official location to MI to simplify her taxes and operations. I suggested that Melanie talk with someone at LARA about the best way to do this. I have Iron Horse listed as Not Yet in Business because I expect they will become In Business in MI this year.


Melanie will also be assisting Dana form a new single member LLC (DMS Consulting, if it's available) in MI. We don't have an eRFC for that one, and I need to find out what I'll need to get the start.

She operates, with her husband, a youth mentoring business using basketball training as its methodology. We completed her 502 form and submitted it to LARA. She noted her local team competed in a multi-state tournament and won!!

Follow up notes from our call - Next Steps

Lindsay

Thank you for your time today. Congratulations on finding the PT job that fits with your future business goals. Smart!


I've attached our business plan template - please consider it an outline of questions. You may want to simply incorporate some of the information into your existing plan vs. starting from scratch. 


Our next steps:

  • Our team will take your financial information and expand it into the 36-month model and we can discuss it further once I forward it to you
  • You'll complete the written part of your business plan and we can plan to review this in our next session
  • After our next session, we can book a separate session to file your LLC, which would only take about 10 minutes to complete

In the meantime, have a wonderful day and weekend!

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Summary: The client reached out through Maggie McCammon for questions. To provide some background, this SBIR Grant (Topic # RFA-MD-24-007) is the one he referenced for support. He has the goal to help one million people quit vaping by 2027, given his own struggle with vaping addiction as a teenager. While at MSU, he created CAPNOS to address sensory dependencies with the use of flavored pressurized air inhalers as behavioral alternatives for cravings. Since launching three years ago, they have shipped over 85,000 devices to over 93 countries supporting customers in their harm reduction journeys. An R21 application is being reviewed this month to assess the efficacy of their devices with human lab studies, submitted by Northwestern University. He would like to better understand resources and pathways with these topics: Health education in middle and high schools: curriculum for vaping/smoking reduction. Goal: adjust fear-based messaging to help students better understand habits and u

Mr. Gomez

Thank you very much for your time today. The expansion of your business is really exciting. I can see where you can increase the business to area restaurants as well as the expansion into other states.


Some more details:

  1. The Basic Inc.Exp is where we start inputting the numbers from years 2023 and 2024, You can create 2 different files if you wish. Then please send them back to me when completed
  2. The MISBDC simple business plan template is what we discussed and you can use whatever you created with Northern Initiatives rather than start all over again
  3. The Financial projections - we will take what you share with us in the Basic Inc.Exp and then input it into this model. This is just for reference - NO NEED TO DO ANYTHING on this one


More resources:

Grant through BCFR - please view this website

Summary/Action: The client and I had a phone call to discuss his NSF project pitch and how he can make corrections to better increase his chances to get an invitation on next submission. We discussed the specifics of the pitch, like how most of his innovation is actually in his challenges and his current innovation is more market innovation instead of the technical innovation that they are looking for. We discussed the specifics of NSFs definition of innovation and I encouraged him to connect with his new technical PI to go over it and how this could be reconstructed to follow that definition. I showed him the parts in the challenges and explained why they better fit the innovation section. We talked about what to describe as far as market and to leave out the “high-end” clientele in this pitch as they won’t call a big enough market. We talked about restructuring the team section as well.


Plan: He will go to talk to his PI and start work on a new draft and send it

Prior to the meeting I sent Cam links to Entity Search and Online for filing for an LLC. He is interested in a single person mobile welding, fabrication and repair business.

Asked questions to get some background information on why he wants to start a small single-person welding & fabrication business. We did an entity search and the name CK Welding and Fabrication appeared to be available. (his first choice of CK Fabrication was not available). I walked him through the registration process for an LLC. He said he will look it over and file the form 700 himself.

  • He says he does not need financing as he already has a truck, welder, and related equipment.
  • Advised he contact his insurance agent regarding any insurance he will need.
  • Went over the basic business plan. He will work on thios
  • Asked him about bookkeeping. He will need to find a bookkeeper or learn to do it himself. (Quickbooks?)

He will work on the Basic Business

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: I spoke with our client by telephone. Not much happening from his front. Business has been slow and he has been focused mostly upon his day job. Will reach out to him in 4 months.

Met with Erik and heard about his existing business Computer Dust Solutions and a planned entity Buychickens.com.


Proposing a new venture BridgeUP.AI. It's a business idea to utilize AI to automate certain business functions for service-based businesses. Two discussed were various contractors as well as healthcare providers such as chiropractors. For example, the business solution would assist with front desk, customer service and scheduling tasks to free up the professional to concentrate on their core services. Lots of discussion on his idea, business model and target customers.

The main outcome was a suggestion that Erik find one potential pilot customer to utilize his solution. Erik can then observe how his solution serves the customers needs, identify pains points, and look for ways to improve his solution.

I will follow up in a few weeks to see where he is at in the process.

I called our client and left a message seeking follow up with some of the decisions they had to make: with or without builder's license and the type of jobs he would be able to take on, single or multimember LLC to name two important decisions.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Hi Siera: 


I apologize for the delayed response and appreciate you for checking in with me. I just returned home from Chicago. 


Regarding my business, yes, I have refined my focus from general plants to medicinal plants. Specifically, integrating Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) Practices. My approach uses medicinal plants as both a remedy and food. My products consist of medicinal herbal oils, heating pads, a symptom-tracking notebook, a diet-tracking notebook, and cooking courses. It is a system designed to address two chronic health issues I have personally experienced: shoulder pain and postnasal drip. 


Currently, I am in the production phase and plan to launch my store before Mother's Day. In addition, I will be hosting an online interview with a well-known Chinese TCM doctor to discuss postnasal drip treatment at the beginning of May. Once I complete the produ

Our client contacted me having trouble accessing her LivePlan account. This has happened before as she had opened her own account after we had given her access to LP. I asked how her meeting with her attorney went. She met with Zachary Everitt of Galagher, Everitt & Associates, PLLC in Alma to review her lease after the fact. It was a fruitful and enlightening meeting for her and will hopefully lead to an early resolution of some landlord issues that have come up.


She mentioned that she can now accept EBT payments for food. She inquired about the OTC benefit for Medicare and Medicaid recipients but it seems that only large chains have been allowed to accept that benefit card. I will check around.


She intends to pursue loans and grants in addition to the MDARD grant she was awarded for $100K. We talk

Hi Elizabeth,


I hope that you had a good weekend!

Karmel owns a laundromat in our region, and I think she sent in an intake form at the tail end of the first application cycle. However, she is ready to explore the possibility now! Client is in process of acquiring an existing floral business (flower shop in Fowlerville, MI). She is fairly far along in the process -- has a purchase agreement, an agreed price, and is in process of arranging financing. The bank wants to see a business plan including 3 years of projections, which is where the client is looking for help. See attached email communication:



Corri – nice to meet you this morning. Sorry to bombard your inbox, but I am including some info:


As discussed, here is a very simple tool we use for business plan creation called Lean Canvas. It may give you some ideas on how to organize your thoughts. 


Some ideas / topics you may want to include in your write up:

  • Your vision for the business and it’s success
  • Any market research you’ve done --
  • As discussed, ideas to ‘modernize’ business (ie social media, new products, new customer segments, etc)
  • Competition and an

Client has contracted with a strategic planning consultant and they wanted my input for their development session. I informed the client that I offer that same service through SBDC and that she did not need to spend the consultant fee of $2000. She wanted both consultants to work together. The paid consultant wanted my input before the session and for me to observe during the session (not join or talk). I declined the offer to attend and will work with the client after she receives her plan. We will then take the strategic plan and incorporate it into a business plan.

Analysis: Karen is still at a similar stage in her progress. She has had discussions with the attorney and is currently awaiting a fee estimate. She believes that engaging an attorney for legal protection, particularly concerning her work in journalism, is a prudent step. She is still in the process of preparing for her business launch.

Recommendation: Karen should continue to focus on developing her graphic design skills to ensure she is fully prepared for the launch. Heather,


I was thinking about this over the weekend. It has come up for you a few times with different owners. I wonder what it would be like to have a response "ready to go" when you get questions about being exclusive. What would an exclusive partnership look like? Would it be based on geographic miles? Sales volumes? Store types? Product types?


Instead of saying "I can't accommodate having an exclusive relationship with your store," maybe lay out parameters on how you can be exclusive. Then you could review those relationships and parameters periodically, like yearly.


Just some thoughts.



Daniel Yoder

Analysis: Malinda initially focused her efforts on candles but has since decided to broaden her business scope to include a variety of products. While candles will remain a central offering, she plans to expand her product line. In light of this shift, we have established an LLC for her business to facilitate its online operations.

Recommendation: To maximize her business potential, it is essential for Malinda to maintain a clear focus on her primary brand and work towards building strong brand awareness around it. Once this foundation is established, she can then gradually expand by creating subdomains for her additional product offerings. This approach will allow her to maintain brand cohesion while diversifying her product range.

Joslynn contacted with an interest in forming a mobile bar. Michigan doesn't issue liquor licenses to mobile bars. This means you cannot purchase and sell alcohol yourself. However, the law appears to allow bars to serve clients whereby the client purchases and provides the alcohol for to mix and serve. This would be in the case of weddings and private events, etc. So she would not be able to purchase and serve alcohol herself.

Joslynn is interested in pursuing this option. I forwarded to her a few links on the subject, including one for the Michigan Liquor Control Commission. I advised her to contact them to find out what she can.

Major issues:

  • She is thinking about getting a trailer that she could bring to events for the purpose of serving. I suggested that since many places would already have some kind of bar setup, she could look into basically being a free-lance bartender. She was quite responsive to this idea.

Yes, those three goals—natural, sustainable, and affordable—can be compatible, but there are some trade-offs and strategy needed. Here's a quick breakdown of how they fit and where tension might show up:


✅ Why They Can Work Together:

Natural ingredients like oils, clays, herbs, and essential oils are often inexpensive and accessible—especially if made in small batches or sourced smartly.


Sustainability doesn’t always mean expensive; using minimal packaging, recyclable materials, or local ingredients can help keep costs down.


Many customers are actively looking for brands that balance values with budget—so there’s a good market fit.


⚠️ Where Tension Might Arise:

Truly sustainable packaging (e.g., glass jars, compostable labels, zero plastic) often costs more than traditional options.

By invitation from Jacqueline Jones Love, I attended the initial meeting with the two founders of this startup operating currently out of WSU Techtown. Rodney Dickerson and Babatunde Ajao are developing a software program which will assist Detroit based small businesses to select a good location for their restaurant/retail store, etc. based on a database of demographics, zoning, foot traffic, crime rate, etc. They are working with Data Driven Detroit for some of the information they need. Next step: Jacqueline will receive the written business plan and contact the Tech Team about this start-up.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Summary: The DoT sent the client an update amendment to the solicitation that they just applied for. She is worried that this affects their funding and wanted to know what it meant.


Action: I looked over the document and I responded that I suspect that this is all cost-cutting. All three of these changes outlined will save them program administrative money which I am guessing they have been tasked to do. They will no longer have a pitch day meaning that the client will either get the award based on this application or they won’t. Previously they have offered a “pitch day” to what I suspect were their bubble awardees – those that were too close in ranking to decide between. They will not do that this year. Further, they formerly offered an opportunity to respond to their review comments and try to change their minds based on a rebuttal document. They were the only agency that allowed this and they will no longer do so. Finally, they will not contract with a business

Started research on compare/contrast AI tools that can help with a UI clickthrough, here is a list (beyond figma but reviewed latest:


Uizard's core value proposition: It's an AI-powered tool designed to rapidly turn ideas (from hand-drawn sketches, wireframes, text prompts, or screenshots) into editable digital UI mockups, prototypes, and even front-end code snippets. It aims to democratize design, making it accessible for non-designers, product managers, founders, and anyone needing to visualize concepts quickly.

List of Uizard.io Competitors:

Competitors fall into several categories:

  1. Direct AI UI Generation Competitors: Tools with a similar AI-driven approach to generating UI from various inputs.
    • Visily: Focuses on converting screenshots and sketches into editable mockups and themes. Strong emphasis on AI assisting the design process.

Received email from Sara, and spoke with her on the phone.

  • She received word today from MSUFCU that they are not going to finance her building/renovation project due to not being comfortable with the industry she is in.
  • She is obviously upset, as she is very near the point of submitting the grant application for the RAP grant or the Community Revitalization grant and she was expecting a term sheet.
  • She asked about other banks for financing, and I referred her to Commercial Bank, Mercantile Bank, Isabella Bank, and also Northern Initiatives. She had Corey's number but I told her I would send her contact info for the rest.

Spoke at length with Vince Nystrom about this business. The company's secure software uses advanced data science, and AI to identify opportunities for successful health insurance appeals and generate custom appeal letters. Seems like a very promising company.

Meeting Summary:

The meeting brought together representatives from Ann Arbor SPARK (Sara Ccat), Automation Alley (John Baez), and the SBDC Tech/BAF team (Nancy Benovich Gilby, Clay Phillips, Toni Brownfield, Alain Piette) to establish clear roles, responsibilities, and expectations for supporting the entrepreneur Mario and his two distinct companies, Drone Pilot Academy and Aerobots.

  1. Purpose: Alain convened the meeting to ensure a coordinated approach, avoid conflicting messages to the client, and confirm adherence to SBDC/BAF program rules, given Mario's engagement with multiple SmartZones/contacts.
  2. SmartZone Lead & Client Management: It was definitively agreed that Ann Arbor SPARK (represented by Sara Ccat) will be the lead SmartZone and primary point of contact for Mario moving forward. Sara confirmed her familiarity with Mario and his ventures and her willingness

CURRENT STATUS

They remain focused on the diligent integration of DME Licensing, clinical trials and venture capital application... I have nurtured earnest readiness and willingness from Keith Berry at United FCU to explore ways to fund some of the operations. (We met last week, His most recent email is at the bottom). I prompted a general inquiry with the principals at Kalamazoo Forward as they are seeking ventures to fund - We will meet this Wednesday.


In the meantime, Samuel has affirmed that they prefer remain focused prior to engaging another lender. I will leave it at bay for now....review again in 7-10 days.

B - Talked about how to scale slowly. Getting client feedback on design and what they would buy. Order a few extras, but keep inventory low until understanding what people want. Get clients to take a photo and post on their own sites and hashtag his company.


A - Wants info on how to set up LLC and EIN, but shared he paid a company to do it. Want to see him develop a full business plan so that he is clear about direction and scalability.


R - Study Daymon John. He bagan FUBU for exactly the same reason Jafar wants to create a clothing brand for Shia Muslims.
